content:
Welcome back to the lesson. This session will be a little bit shorter and more direct to the point. We'll be
looking at some examples, building on what was shown on Tuesday night. We will analyze Wednesday's
and Thursday's trading.

- INITIAL SETUP
First up is the E-mini S&P March delivery contract for 2022, specifically Wednesday, January 26th. You
want to take a look at the low over here. It's crucial to not just use my charts, but to look at your own charts
from your platform or broker, whatever feed you're using. The example uses TradingView, which offers end
- of-day data for free, or real-time data for a small fee.

You want to identify the low on Wednesday the 26th and the subsequent high. This price run from that low
to that high defines your range. You then drop your FIB on that range to get your equilibrium price point.

As the highs were run through, the market gets pushed into a premium, making it really expensive. We
then wait to see if it breaks down below the old high. It does, but does it create a fair value gap in that
area? No, it doesn't. It rallies one more time, then breaks down again. Inside this structure, there's a break
in market structure. We see it here. Then, price runs back up into the fair value gap here and then sells off,
reaching into this old low, which would be below the equilibrium price point of this entire range, or the 50
percent level on the FIB.

Let's put some 'lipstick' on this again. Here's the low and the high. Equilibrium is the 50 percent level. We're
looking for a low or an imbalance below this level. We have a swing low here and an imbalance here that
could be a target. Notice how price hits it and comes off of it, then finds a more pronounced low here and
draws down into that. Inside the shaded blue area, we'll take a closer look at the details.

We're zoomed in on a 2-minute chart. The previous high, a short-term high, at 8:30 in the morning, starts
the hunt. We're looking for an old high to be violated, which it does here. Then, there's a break in market
structure. This swing low candle over here sets the stage. Now, you have an opportunity: does price draw
back up into the fair value gap after this candle breaks below here, giving you a bearish market structure
shift? Yes, it trades up into the fair value gap.

section_title: ENTRY, TARGET, AND STUDY JOURNAL GUIDANCE (ES)
content:
You can go short there. You could target this old low or that low that was annotated before we zoomed in.
Observe the beautiful delivery. A hypothetical short from here and a hypothetical cover here would be
logged in your study journal. Do not use these examples alone; go into your own charts and annotate them
as well. Shorting around 4419 and covering at 4382 is an example, not striving for perfection. It's still a very
nice delivery based on the logic taught previously.

section_title: DETAILED ENTRY CONSIDERATIONS AND PATTERN RECOGNITION (ES)
content:
Again, there's that fair value gap, and that's your candle that would be considered an ideal entry. The
discount old low is a target right there. Your charts would look just like this. In any open space, you would
just type in whatever important factors you found insightful about this example. Ask yourself these
questions:
- How long did it take?
- How long did it take from the market structure shift occurring to get up into the fair value gap? That's real
important.
- How long did it take before it went from your entry down to your target in time?
- How much heat or drawdown would you have weathered getting short?
If you used this candle's high as your entry point, you technically could have got filled here. I want to teach
you to look at the total range available and in hindsight, because it gives you a framework to look for how
these examples, while they're not exactly the same, they don't repeat exactly, there are a lot of similarities
that you'll find that are reoccurring. That's the important factor to train your eye to see it. Remember the
analogy about the deer track: you have to know what a deer track looks like to stalk it effectively. This is
your pattern and setup, so you need to go in looking for it to reinforce the idea that you know what it looks
like.

- INITIAL 5-MINUTE ANALYSIS
Now we're looking at the E-mini Nasdaq futures for Thursday, January 27th, 2022. This is a 5-minute chart,
which is the timeframe you start with and work down from. On the 5-minute, right away we can see the low
to the high. We're looking for a high; go back in time in your chart, and you'll find what this is here. You also
have these relative equal highs, which could have been an initial objective. You look for these relative equal
highs to be taken out. Does it break down and give you a fair value gap? No, it doesn't, so it wouldn't even
meet the criteria there. I know some of you might be looking to say, 'Well, how do you know it doesn't get
you on a trade here?' Listen to the rules I gave you; they're very specific. That's what I'm walking you
through here, allowing the market, or really forcing the market, to show you its hand. Once it does that,
then you can go in and act accordingly, but you have to wait for the setup. You can't force it or think it's
there; it's there or it's not.

section_title: WAITING FOR MARKET CONFIRMATION AND ENTRY (NQ)
content:
We have an old high here with buy-side liquidity resting above that. We have a run above it. Once it goes
above here, does it go above it and break down and then go down below that short-term low there? No, it
goes higher. So, we just keep waiting for a break lower to get a shift in the market structure on an intraday
basis. We have a swing low here. It trades down through it on this candle here. So, right away, at that
moment, on this candle, as soon as this candle trades through it, you now have an opportunity. Then, you
go back into the high to that candle low, and do you see a fair value gap? It's right there. This market rallies
back up, trades into it here; you can be a short seller there.

section_title: TARGETING AND CONCLUDING REMARKS (NQ & NEXT STEPS)
content:
The market breaks down one more time, re-trades back into it, and reaches below the equilibrium price
point. You have a couple of targets here: you could be here, here, or in the imbalance, and it hits all of them
and goes a little bit further lower. That was quick and hopefully painless. Again, we're looking at internal
range liquidity and market structure shifts intraday. My advice is for you to go back through more data, use
the coming weekend, and acquire more of those examples so that way you can include them in your study
journal. Next week, I'll work on targeting refinement and entries. Until then, enjoy your weekend and be
safe.
